'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).

He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.

Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. [[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />

Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.

These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe-->[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe-->[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[ko:%EC%98%A4%EC%8B%9C%EC%82%AC%EC%B9%B4%EB%85%B8%ED%9E%88%EC%BD%94%ED%9E%88%ED%86%A0%EB%85%B8%EC%98%A4%EC%97%90%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[zh:%E6%8A%BC%E5%9D%82%E5%BD%A5%E4%BA%BA%E5%A4%A7%E5%85%84%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]]
[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disaqqearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial suqqort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
