[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disa11earance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial su11ort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] <!--Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe-->[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] <!--Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe-->[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[ko:%EC%98%A4%EC%8B%9C%EC%82%AC%EC%B9%B4%EB%85%B8%ED%9E%88%EC%BD%94%ED%9E%88%ED%86%A0%EB%85%B8%EC%98%A4%EC%97%90%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[zh:%E6%8A%BC%E5%9D%82%E5%BD%A5%E4%BA%BA%E5%A4%A7%E5%85%84%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]]
[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disa11earance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial su11ort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] <!--Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe-->[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[Category:11]] <!--Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe-->[[Category:Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe]][[ko:%EC%98%A4%EC%8B%9C%EC%82%AC%EC%B9%B4%EB%85%B8%ED%9E%88%EC%BD%94%ED%9E%88%ED%86%A0%EB%85%B8%EC%98%A4%EC%97%90%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[zh:%E6%8A%BC%E5%9D%82%E5%BD%A5%E4%BA%BA%E5%A4%A7%E5%85%84%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90]]
[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disa11earance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial su11ort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappear[[Category:11 to pp]]ance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial su11ort for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappear[[Category:pp to pp]]ance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappear[[Category:pp to pp]]ance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references />== See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappear[[Category:pp to pp]]ance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappear[[Category:pp to pp]]ance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

[[ja:押坂彦人大兄皇子]] {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}} | 父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappear[[Category:pp to pp]]ance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎}}

<!--Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe--> <!--Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe--> {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=draft}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=draft}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=draft}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=draft}} | 子女 = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=draft}} {{ill|#Genealogy|and others}}|父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=draft}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|12=draft}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|ja|広姫|12=draft}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=draft}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=draft}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=draft}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=draft}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|assassinated|ja|暗殺|12=draft}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=draft}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=draft}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=draft}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|12=draft}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=draft}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=draft}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=draft}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=draft}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=draft}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=draft}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=draft}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=draft}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 =不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = [[糠手姫皇女]] | 配偶者2 = [[大俣女王]] | 配偶者3 = [[桜井弓張皇女]] | 配偶者4 = [[小墾田皇女]] | 配偶者5 = | 配偶者6 = | 配偶者7 = | 配偶者8 = | 配偶者9 = | 配偶者10 = | 子女 ='''[[舒明天皇]]'''、[[茅渟王]] [[#系譜|ほか]] | 父親 ='''[[敏達天皇]]''' | 母親 = [[広姫]] | 役職 = }} '''押坂彦人大兄皇子'''（おしさかのひこひとのおおえのみこ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。'''押坂彦人皇子'''、'''麻呂古皇子'''、'''太子彦人皇子'''、'''忍坂日子人太子'''、'''皇祖大兄'''とも。'''[[敏達天皇]]'''の第一皇子で、母は[[広姫]]。子に'''[[舒明天皇]]'''、[[茅渟王]]がいる。
今日の[[皇室]]は、押坂彦人大兄皇子の男系子孫にあたる。
== 生涯 == 敏達天皇の第一皇子として誕生。母は[[息長真手王]]の娘・広姫。
[[蘇我氏]]の血を引かない敏達王統の最有力者であって、忍坂部（[[刑部氏]]）・丸子部などの独立した財政基盤を有し、王都を離れて水派宮（みまたのみや、[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）を営んでいた。[[用明天皇]]の[[崩御]]（[[587年]]）後に王位継承者として候補に挙がったとされるが、対立する蘇我系王族が台頭したため、以後の史料には活動が一切見えず、蘇我氏によって[[暗殺]]されたとの憶測<ref>[[587年]]の蘇我氏と[[物部氏]]の争いの最中に暗殺（[[山尾幸久]]）ないしは横死（[[和田萃]]）したのではないかとする推測があるが、史料的根拠はなく、系譜の生年とも矛盾するため、少数説となっている。</ref>もある。ただし、『[[一代要記]]』や『[[本朝皇胤紹運録|紹運録]]』を基に逆算される舒明の生年（[[593年]]）とその弟の存在を考えると、[[592年]]の[[推古天皇]]即位後も暫く生存していたはずで、非蘇我系の王位継承候補者として、蘇我系の[[竹田皇子]]や[[聖徳太子|厩戸皇子]]と比肩し得る地位を保っていたと思われる。[[607年]]に王位継承者（厩戸）を資養する壬生部（みぶべ）が設置されているので、これ以前には亡くなっていたらしい。
『[[延喜式]]』諸陵寮には「成相墓（ならひのはか）。押坂彦人大兄皇子。在大和国廣瀬郡。兆域東西廿五町。南北廿町。守戸五烟」とあり、記載のとおりとすればその規模は現在日本最大の[[大仙陵古墳]]（同じく『延喜式』に「兆域東西八町。南北八町。陵戸五烟。」とある）の10倍以上の広さがあったことになる。墓陵には現在の奈良県[[広陵町]]の[[牧野古墳]]を推定する研究がある。
忍坂部や丸子部といった押坂彦人大兄皇子伝来の私領は「皇祖大兄御名入部」と呼ばれ、以後も息子である舒明から孫の中大兄皇子（後の[[天智天皇]]）らへと引き継がれて、[[大化の改新]]後に国家に返納された（『[[日本書紀]]』[[大化]]2年[[3月20日_(旧暦)|3月壬午]]条）と考えられており、彦人大兄の死後においても、皇子の系統が蘇我氏や上宮王家に対抗して舒明即位から大化の改新の実現を可能にしたのは、こうした財政的裏付けの存在があったからだと言われている。
== 系譜 == 主として『[[古事記]]』に拠る。 * 妃：[[小墾田皇女]]（おはりたのひめみこ。敏達天皇・[[推古天皇]]の皇女） * 妃：[[桜井弓張皇女]]（さくらいのゆみはりのひめみこ、桜井玄王。敏達天皇・推古天皇の皇女。小墾田皇女の同母妹。婚姻前もしくは押坂彦人大兄皇子の死後[[来目皇子]]妃か） ** 山代王（やましろのみこ） ** 笠縫王（かさぬいのみこ） * 妃：[[糠手姫皇女]]（ぬかでひめのみこ、宝王・田村皇女。敏達天皇の皇女） ** 田村王（たむらのみこ）（[[舒明天皇]]） ** 中津王（なかつみこ） ** [[多良王]]（たらのみこ） * 妃：[[大俣女王]]（おおまたのみこ。[[漢王]]の妹） ** [[茅渟王]]（ちぬのみこ、智奴王。宝皇女（'''[[斉明天皇|皇極、斉明天皇]]'''）・'''[[孝徳天皇]]'''の父） ** 桑田王（くわたのみこ、女性）
4人の妃が確認されるが、うち3人は押坂彦人大兄皇子にとって異母姉妹に当たる。
なお、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京皇別に敏達天皇の孫として見える「[[百済王 (皇族)|百済王]]（くたらのみこ）」も彦人大兄皇子の子であることは確実だが、『古事記』に掲げられた系譜には見えないため、多良王（久多良王の久が脱落）や茅渟王と同一人に考える説がある。
{{女性天皇系図}}
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[押坂部毛屎]]…皇子の従者の1人と推定される。
{{DEFAULTSORT:おしさかのひこひとのおおえのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:敏達天皇と推古天皇の子女|広姫おしさか]] [[Category:6世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:7世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:6世紀生]] [[Category:没年不明]]

今日の[[皇室]]は、押坂彦人大兄皇子の男系子孫にあたる。

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|qq=}}

}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|qq=}}

{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11497661]] Wikidata name: Prince Oshisaka-no-Hikohito-no-Ōe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|qq=}}

He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=}}.

After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).

The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
