Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata

== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|qq=|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|qq=|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|qq=|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|qq=|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|qq=|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|qq=|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|qq=|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|qq=|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata

== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata

However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497661]] Wikidata name: Prince Oshisaka-no-Hikohito-no-Ōe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497661]] Wikidata name: Prince Oshisaka-no-Hikohito-no-Ōe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497661]] Wikidata name: Prince Oshisaka-no-Hikohito-no-Ōe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{ill|Prince Kasuga|WD=Q11513657}}; {{ill|Prince Naniha|WD=Q11659560}}; {{ill|Prince Ohomata|WD=Q11437415}}; {{ill|Prince Takeda|WD=Q11599359}}; {{ill|Prince Wohari|WD=Q11620912}}; {{ill|Princess Tame|WD=Q11577210}}; {{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497661]] Wikidata name: Prince Oshisaka-no-Hikohito-no-Ōe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|ja|押坂部毛屎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|1=Oshisaka no Hikohito's Retainers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923201}}

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497661]] Wikidata name: Prince Oshisaka-no-Hikohito-no-Ōe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
