{{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497661]] Wikidata name: Prince Oshisaka-no-Hikohito-no-Ōe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' | 母親 = [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]] | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''[[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]''' and his consort, [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]]. His children include '''[[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]]''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]], his mother was [[:en:Hirohime|Hirohime]], daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] in [[:en:587|587]], he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was [[:en:Assassination|assassinated]]<ref>Speculations include assassination during the [[:en:587|587]] conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after [[:en:592|592]], during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]].
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]] (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566}}}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Ichidai Yōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{ill|Prince Wohari|WD=Q11620912|1=Prince Wohari}}; {{ill|Princess Tame|WD=Q11577210|1=Princess Tame}}; {{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070|1=Princess Uji no Shitsukahi}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566|1=Woharida}}}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587|1=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated|1=Assassination}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587|1=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Q3147644|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592|1=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms|1=Taika Reform}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587|1=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated|1=Assassination}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587|1=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Q3147644|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592|1=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms|1=Taika Reform}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

{{ill|Prince Wohari|WD=Q11620912|1=Prince Wohari}}; {{ill|Princess Tame|WD=Q11577210|1=Princess Tame}}; {{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukahi|WD=Q10509070|1=Princess Uji no Shitsukahi}}; {{ill|Woharida|WD=Q11459566|1=Woharida}}}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 押坂彦人大兄皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 敏達天皇第1皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 成相墓 | 配偶者1 = {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} | 配偶者2 = {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 配偶者3 = {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} | 配偶者4 = {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}} | issue = '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}''', {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}} [[#Genealogy|and others]]|父親 = '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' | 母親 = {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}} | 役職 = }}
'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587|1=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated|1=Assassination}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587|1=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Q3147644|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592|1=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms|1=Taika Reform}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.

'''Shōshoi''' (少初位), also known as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito no Ōe no Miko''' (押坂彦人大兄皇子, [[生没年不詳]]), was a member of the imperial family during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. He was also referred to as '''Oshisaka no Hikohito Prince''', '''Maroko Prince''', '''Taishi Hikohito Prince''', '''Oshisaka Hikohito Taishi''', and '''Ōso Taishi'''. He was the first son of '''{{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}''' and his consort, {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}. His children include '''{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}''' and {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}.
The modern {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} is descended in the male line from Shōshoi.
== Life == Born as the first son of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}, his mother was {{ill|Hirohime|en|Hirohime|WD=Q11064567|lt=Hirohime|1=Hirohime}}, daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Makoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Makoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}}.
Shōshoi was a prominent figure of the Bidatsu lineage, which was unconnected to the Soga clan. He maintained independent financial bases such as the {{ill|Oshisaka|ja|忍坂部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Maroko|ja|丸子部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maroko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸子氏|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368098}} estates and resided outside the royal capital at the Mimata Palace (水派宮), likely located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|奈良県河合町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} in {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587|1=587}}, he was considered a potential heir to the throne. However, the rise of the Soga clan resulted in his disappearance from historical records. Some speculate he was {{ill|Assassination|en|Assassination|WD=Q3882219|lt=assassinated|1=Assassination}}<ref>Speculations include assassination during the {{ill|587|en|587|WD=Q30891|lt=587|1=587}} conflict between the Soga and Mononobe clans ({{ill|Yukihisa Yamano|ja|山尾幸久|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihisa Yamano|1=Yukihisa Yamao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467776}}) or an accidental death ({{ill|Hajime Wada|ja|和田萃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajime Wada|1=Atsumu Wada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418120}}), but there is no historical evidence.</ref>. Nevertheless, based on records in the ''{{ill|Ichidai Yōki|ja|一代要記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichidai Yōki|1=Q3147644|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3147644}}'' and ''{{ill|Shōunroku|ja|紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} (593), it is believed Shōshoi survived until after {{ill|592|en|592|WD=Q32774|lt=592|1=592}}, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}.
His private estates, including those inherited by his son Emperor Jomei, provided significant financial support for non-Soga claimants to the throne. These estates, called "Imperial Grand Progenitor Named Divisions," were later returned to the state following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms|1=Taika Reform}} (645).
== Genealogy == Based on the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'': * Consort: {{ill|Ohari Princess|ja|小墾田皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohari Princess|1=Woharida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459566}}, daughter of Emperor Bidatsu and Empress Suiko * Consort: {{ill|Sakurai Yumihari Princess|ja|桜井弓張皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Yumihari Princess|1=Sakurai no Yumihari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92923028}} ''' Children: Prince Yamashiro, Prince Kasanu * Consort: {{ill|Nukatehime Princess|ja|糠手姫皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatehime Princess|1=Nukate-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604780}} ''' Children: {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|田村王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jomei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Prince Nakatsu, {{ill|Prince Tara|ja|多良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Consort: {{ill|Ōmata Princess|ja|大俣女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata Princess|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, sister of {{ill|Prince Kan|ja|漢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Children: {{ill|Prince Chinu|ja|茅渟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Chinu|1=Chinu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617199}}, Princess Kuwata
=== Descendants === Shōshoi's descendants, including Emperor Jomei, played pivotal roles in the continuation of the non-Soga lineage during the Asuka period.
